verification.notes: Live-verified 2026-04-09. Legal entity Novi Health International LLC, registered 30 N Gould St, Sheridan WY 82801; ops 4131 N Central Expressway #900, Dallas TX 75204. Pricing pulled directly from the landing page on joinnovi.com: 'Same active ingredient as Ozempic® and Wegovy®. from $174' and 'Same active ingredient as Mounjaro® and Zepbound® from $283'. Three-month commitment clause from ToS: 'the first month's fee as well as the monthly fee for the following two months (for a total of three (3) consecutive months) is charged at the time of purchase and is non-refundable.' No FDA warning letters found for Novi Health International LLC in the FDA warning letters database as of 2026-04-09. Katalys program ID 1550, default payout $200.00. ATTRIBUTION NOTE 2026-04-09: Demoted from the Editor's Pick test on 2026-04-09. The track.revoffers.com redirect for Novi drops ALL Katalys tracking params before reaching joinnovi.com — final landing URL is a bare https://www.joinnovi.com/offer-v6-low with NO offer_id, NO publisher_id, and critically NO transaction_id in the query string. Compare to Eden (Katalys 1435), which lands on https://www.tryeden.com/offer/a-glp1-m?utm_source=Katalys&offer_id=1435&publisher_id=12086&transaction_id=... with the full param set and is CONFIRMED attributing correctly (user-verified 2026-04-09). Per Katalys Thank-You Page Tracking docs verbatim: 'transaction_id is missing: You didn't implement Landing Page tracking correctly.' Without transaction_id on the landing page, even a correctly-installed Katalys tracking script on Novi's side would have nothing to bind the click session to. Likely root cause: Novi's destination URL template in Tune is missing the {transaction_id}/{offer_id}/{publisher_id} macros, OR there's a redirect between the Katalys handoff and the hero page that strips query params. NOT fixable from our publisher side — advertiser must update their Tune config. Filing a support ticket with Katalys is the path forward. Keeping the entry in the dataset with is_featured=false and the affiliate_url intact so the CTAs still work for anyone who lands on the direct provider page; when Novi fixes their tracking we can promote back into the hero.

fda_warnings.description: FDA Warning Letter 717987 issued to FitRX, LLC dba FitRx (the legal entity behind Zealthy) for marketing compounded semaglutide and tirzepatide on fitrxapp.com with false or misleading claims under FDCA Sections 502(a) and 502(bb). FDA cited representations such as 'semaglutide (active ingredient in Wegovy)' and 'tirzepatide (active ingredient in Zepbound)' on compounded, non-FDA-approved products, plus product labeling that suggested FitRx as the compounder when it was not. Letter URL: https://www.fda.gov/inspections-compliance-enforcement-and-criminal-investigations/warning-letters/fitrx-llc-dba-fitrx-717987-02202026

Earlier FDA enforcement action against FitRX/Zealthy reported as part of a broader September 2025 cease-and-desist sweep targeting telehealth marketers of compounded GLP-1s after FDA removed semaglutide from the shortage list on 2025-02-21 (compounding pharmacies lost legal authorization to bulk-compound semaglutide post-shortage). Specific letter ID not publicly indexed at time of verification — confirmed via secondary legal coverage; primary FDA URL pending.

fda_warnings.description: FDA issued a warning letter to Direct Meds on 2025-09-09 as part of an enforcement action that sent warning letters to 55 online telehealth companies / websites selling 'unapproved and misbranded' compounded GLP-1 drugs (semaglutide and tirzepatide). Specific violations cited in the Direct Meds letter include marketing compounded formulations with claims such as 'Same active ingredient as Ozempic and Wegovy' and 'Clinically proven ingredients,' which the FDA held to be false or misleading representations that the compounded product is equivalent to an FDA-approved drug. Source: https://www.aha.org/news/headline/2025-09-17-fda-discloses-warning-letters-dozens-drug-companies

pros: Both semaglutide and tirzepatide available in injection and sublingual formats
All-inclusive pricing with medication, consultations, supplies, and 1–2 day temperature-controlled shipping
Named 503A pharmacy partners: CraftedRx (Warrenton, MO) and ChemistryRx (Folcroft, PA)
cons: FDA warning letter September 9, 2025 for misleading marketing of compounded GLP-1s as equivalent to FDA-approved drugs — compliance status post-warning not publicly documented

Extensive Better Business Bureau complaint pattern (rating 1.12/5 across 226 reviews as of April 2026) documenting a hidden 3-month commitment trap: multiple users report being charged $940+ for a 3-month supply when they believed they were purchasing 1 month. Direct Meds disputes the characterization but the complaint volume is material
Katalys program name 'Direct Meds GLP-1 Offers Top Funnels' and $280 CPA payout indicate aggressive performance-marketing optimization, which can correlate with conversion-first UX patterns
Rating discrepancy: Trustpilot 4.6/5 from 7,500+ reviews vs. BBB 1.12/5 from 226 reviews — suggests either review-solicitation bias or genuine unresolved customer experience issues
Not available in Mississippi, Louisiana, or California
Compounded only — no FDA-approved Ozempic, Wegovy, Zepbound, or Mounjaro
Incorporated May 2024 — less than 2 years of operational history
Auto-renewal continues until manual email cancellation; BBB complaints report retention-department friction and hold times exceeding 15 minutes
best_for: listed for transparent disclosure — see FDA warning and BBB complaint pattern before considering
